title: Permitted types of a sealed class should be omitted if declared in the same file
description: When defining a sealed class in Java, permitted types that are declared in the same file as the sealed class should be omitted from the permits clause. This improves code clarity and reduces redundancy, as the compiler automatically infers permitted types declared in the same file.
recommendation: Omit permitted types from the permits clause if they are declared in the same file as the sealed class.
impact: Redundant permitted types in the permits clause can make code harder to maintain and read.

checks.message: Omit permitted types from the permits clause if they are declared in the same file as the sealed class.
